title: grindelia glutinosa
intro: Grindelia glutinosa, also known as gumweed or sticky gumweed, is a perennial herb native to the western United States, belonging to the daisy family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Grindelia glutinosa include its ability to soothe coughs and colds, reduce fever, and promote healing of skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
